zh.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯
zh.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas是Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar的女兒，於大約1360年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯。她於1379年之前與William Dacre在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1395年在英國波藍德納沃斯享年37歲，埋葬在英國波藍德布藍普頓。

zh-hans.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: 苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas是Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar的女儿，于大约1360年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯。她于1379年之前与William Dacre在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1395年在英国波藍德納沃斯享年37岁，埋葬在英国波藍德布藍普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas was born about 1360, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland as the daughter of Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. She married William Dacre before 1379, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died about 1395, in Naworth, Cumberland, England, at the age of 37, and was buried in Brampton, Cumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas est née environ 1360, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse, fille de Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Elle a épousé William Dacre avant 1379, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1395, à Naworth, Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterrée à Brampton, Cumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas wurde um 1360 in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, als Tochter von Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar geboren. Sie hat vor 1379 in England, United Kingdom William Dacre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1395 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Naworth, Cumberland, England und wurde in Brampton, Cumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas è nata il circa 1360 nel Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland come figlia di Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Lei sposato William Dacre prima del 1379, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1395, nel Naworth, Cumberland, England, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolta a Brampton, Cumberland, England.

ja.Brampton, Cumberland, England: イギリスクウンバーランドブランプトン
ja.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: スコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス
ja.Naworth Castle, Cumberland, England: イギリスクウンバーランドナウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Douglasは約1360年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスでSir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Marの娘として生まれた。1379年以前にイギリスイギリスでWilliam Dacreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1395年にイギリスクウンバーランドナウオルスで37歳で死去し，イギリスクウンバーランドブランプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas(은)는 대략 1360년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1379년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 William Dacre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1395년 영국 쿠벌랜드 나워스에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 쿠벌랜드 브램프턴에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1360–about 1395: tany ho any amin'ny 1360–tany ho any amin'ny 1395

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland filha de Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Ela casou-se com William Dacre antes 1379, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1395, em Naworth, Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 37 anos, e foi sepultada em Brampton, Cumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas родилась около 1360 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия. Её отца звали Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Dacre перед 1379 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1395 г. в местности наворт, кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 37 лет и была похоронена в местности брамптон, кумбирланд, Англия.

sm.about 1360–about 1395: e tusa o le 1360–e tusa o le 1395

es.lifeSummary: Joan Douglas nació aproximadamente 1360, en Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland como hija de Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Se casó con William Dacre antes de 1379, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1395, en Naworth, Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultada en Brampton, Cumberland, Inglaterra.
